rationale: Architecture/blast-radius favors A decisively. (1) Shared-predicate impact: B does NOT reuse the bead-centric shared predicate — it keys off WorktreeOps.List(), a different enumeration axis (worktree-entries vs closed-beads-under-epic). It cannot replace FindOrphanedClosedBeads (R3's obligation backstop covers exactly the worktree-LESS merged/deleted beads B is blind to), so B ADDS a second, divergent detection path alongside the shared one — the precise drift surface the single-gate-home architecture rejects. Strictly more surface, not less. (2) B does not even close the TOCTOU by construction: WorktreeOps.List() is a fresh `git worktree list` shell-out at each of its 5 call sites (:195/:235/:255/:383/:519). The gate (approve, T1) and FinalizeEpic (:383, T2) are two separate invocations — a worktree mutated between them re-introduces a race. True single-enumeration requires capturing the list in approve and threading it into FinalizeEpic's signature: cross-boundary plumbing into the executor's most sensitive method. (3) ADR-0030: WorktreeOps is executor-owned git-I/O; making the enforcement gate reason over worktree enumeration pulls I/O concerns up into approve — friction, not fit. (4) Re-review: B re-opens gate/enumeration design + a fresh G2 desync hunt. A is spec-wording-only, ADR-consistent, and the transient (sub-second, non-operator-controllable, self-recovering, operator's own raw-closed bead) is already bounded by Facts 1/2 pinned RED-on-revert (AC11/AC12). Spec-114 audited-refutation precedent applies cleanly.
rejected: B swaps the shared bead-centric predicate for a divergent worktree path that still shells out List() twice — a new TOCTOU plus a drift surface and ADR-0030 friction — for no by-construction guarantee.
